title: Use RMS As A Support Entry Point To Gather Initial System Information
summary: Open the RMS web application, log in, and use the overview screen as an initial support view to understand current AGV or ATP-related issues before deeper troubleshooting.
when_to_use: Use when a support user receives a report that there is a problem and needs an initial system view in RMS to learn more information about AGV or ATP behavior.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a root-cause or corrective-action procedure; this source only supports using RMS to gather initial information.
Do not assume meanings for RMS fields, indicators, or controls that are not described in this source segment.
Do not use this procedure to bypass OptiSweep control flows or make unsupported direct system changes through APIs.

responsibilities: Capture initial observations for follow-on troubleshooting

safety_notes: This source frames RMS here as a support-facing information-gathering interface.
The source warns that bypassing OptiSweep and interacting outside supported flows can cause systems to get out of sync.
Do not infer unsupported meanings or actions from interface elements not explained in this source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the RMS web application
Site-specific RMS address or unique IP address

system_preconditions: The site RMS address or unique IP address is known
The RMS page is reachable from the user's environment

visual_references.description: Training video frame introducing RMS web access, login, and the overview-screen support context.

steps.title: Access the RMS web application
steps.purpose: Reach the support-facing RMS interface used to gather initial system information.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS web application using the site's RMS address or unique IP address.
steps.expected_result: The RMS web application is reached and ready for login.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS web application cannot be reached from the provided address or IP.

actions.label: Open RMS address
actions.exact_action: Access the RMS web application through the site's RMS address or unique IP address.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS described as a web application accessed through a unique IP address.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if RMS cannot be accessed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users access the RMS address, enter a username and password, and then see the RMS overview screen.
Each site has an Argus server and users access the RMS web application through a unique IP address with username and password.
The purpose of this next section is gonna be what does the RMS look like?

steps.title: Log in to RMS
steps.purpose: Authenticate into RMS so the overview screen can be viewed.
steps.instruction: Log in with the assigned username and password.
steps.expected_result: The user is authenticated into RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: Login succeeds and the user proceeds into RMS.
steps.failure_condition: Login fails or credentials do not allow access.

actions.label: Enter credentials
actions.exact_action: Put in the username and password.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training context describing username and password entry for RMS access.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if login cannot be completed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Once you access that address, you put in the username and password... And this is what you will see. This is the overview screen.
Users access the RMS web application through a unique IP address with username and password.

steps.title: Open and observe the RMS overview screen
steps.purpose: Establish the first support view of the system and observe what RMS presents.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS overview screen and observe the information presented there.
steps.expected_result: The RMS overview screen is visible and available for initial review.
steps.healthy_condition: The overview screen is displayed and can be reviewed.
steps.failure_condition: The overview screen is unavailable, blank, or cannot be viewed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS overview-screen context from the training segment, including the overview area referenced in the candidate.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the overview screen is unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: And this is what you will see. This is the overview screen.
So this is just for anybody who has a problem or gets a call, there's a problem. If they open this page...

steps.title: Use the overview information to understand the issue
steps.purpose: Use RMS as an initial diagnostic entry point to learn more about the reported AGV or ATP issue.
steps.instruction: Use the displayed RMS information to learn more about the AGV or ATP issue, consistent with the training segment's support purpose.
steps.expected_result: The user gains initial understanding of the reported issue from RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The overview provides useful starting information about the system state or issue.
steps.failure_condition: The overview does not provide enough information to continue, or the user cannot interpret the screen based on this source alone.

actions.label: Review displayed information
actions.exact_action: Use it to learn more information about the ATP.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Support-oriented RMS overview context for learning more about ATP or AGV behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if interpretation would require assuming meanings not described in this source.
Escalate if RMS information is insufficient for further diagnosis.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: What kind of feature it can provide to an operator and how you can use it to learn more information about the ATP, right?
If they open this page of what, what can they expect and how can they go to the root of the problem as much as they can through this API, right?

steps.title: Record initial RMS observations
steps.purpose: Preserve the initial system view before moving into deeper troubleshooting.
steps.instruction: Record what the RMS overview shows before moving to deeper troubleshooting, if additional investigation is needed.
steps.expected_result: Initial RMS observations are documented for follow-on troubleshooting.
steps.healthy_condition: The user has captured the initial state seen in RMS.
steps.failure_condition: No initial observations are recorded before deeper investigation begins.

actions.label: Document observations
actions.exact_action: Record what the RMS overview shows before moving to deeper troubleshooting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if additional troubleshooting is needed but the initial RMS state cannot be captured or described from this source-supported view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This is just for anybody who has a problem or gets a call, there's a problem.
How can they use it to learn more information...

success_criteria: The user logs in and reaches the overview screen.
The user gathers initial information about the AGV or ATP issue from the RMS overview.
Initial observations are recorded before deeper troubleshooting.
healthy_conditions: The RMS web application is reachable.
The screen provides an initial support-oriented view of the system.
failure_conditions: The source does not provide enough detail to safely interpret specific fields or indicators.
The source does not provide root-cause logic or corrective actions beyond initial information gathering.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if RMS cannot be accessed or the overview screen is unavailable.
Escalate if login cannot be completed with assigned credentials.
Escalate if deeper troubleshooting is required because this source only supports initial information gathering.
Do not proceed based on assumed meanings for fields or indicators not described in this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS web application access, login context, and overview-screen support framing.
